canonical_paths.name: Cloud progression
canonical_paths.outcome: A reviewed local application reaches Azure, Dataverse, and M365 without exporting private runtime state.

canonical_paths.name: Disaster recovery / offline egg
canonical_paths.outcome: An independently verified offline egg restores an isolated twin before replacement.

canonical_paths.name: Fleet coordination
canonical_paths.outcome: A controller fans bounded signed operations to enrolled nodes and aggregates signed results.

canonical_paths.name: Fresh local agent
canonical_paths.outcome: A reviewed one-file agent becomes an independently discoverable local capability.

canonical_paths.name: Global brainstem to isolated twin
canonical_paths.outcome: The authenticated local owner operates this product, whose controller builds and supervises a child with separate workspace, venv, identity, keys, and state.

canonical_paths.name: Neighborhood sharing
canonical_paths.outcome: A signed event crosses an untrusted relay and is independently verified by an admitted peer.

canonical_paths.name: Owner iMessage to rapplication to reply
canonical_paths.outcome: One enrolled owner DM is rejected or becomes one durable signed request and one durable reply.

canonical_paths.name: Public rapplication publish / hatch
canonical_paths.outcome: Immutable public discovery leads to verified, contained, isolated local installation.

collisions.issue: Host guidance and executable BasicAgents are both called agents/capabilities.
collisions.resolution: Only ABI-conforming reviewed Python enters the agent manifest; skills remain host integration material.

collisions.issue: double-jump and lisppy use incompatible cubby schemas.
collisions.resolution: CUBBY defines one new exact profile and rejects both as implicit substitutes.

collisions.issue: Four lifecycle stages are often flattened into one cartridge/egg concept.
collisions.resolution: The first three are mandatory ordered artifacts; cart remains a non-selected UX contract.

collisions.issue: Behavioral grail authority is mistaken for licensed source authority.
collisions.resolution: Installer and Microsoft are reference-only; the selected project runtime is original clean-room implementation with no adapted external runtime source.

collisions.issue: Fleet coordination and one wrapped multicellular being share the Leviathan name.
collisions.resolution: Model, test, and exclude/select them independently; neither authorizes the other.

collisions.issue: Child twins are sometimes flattened into tools of one mutable global runtime.
collisions.resolution: Controller streams/supervises; each child keeps independent agents, workspace, venv, identity, keys, and state.

collisions.issue: Double Jump strict Moment fields reject Hologram organism fields.
collisions.resolution: No implicit compatibility; use explicit versioned projection only.

collisions.issue: Static front doors and landing pages are presented as if they were running twins.
collisions.resolution: Pages is public static substrate only and is excluded from v1 runtime.

collisions.issue: Species-root/reference distro and consumer runtime are treated as one distribution.
collisions.resolution: Both are reference-only; selected source is Microsoft and selected integration is new.

collisions.issue: Legacy/current widths, canonicalizers, lineage, and display namespaces conflict.
collisions.resolution: One approved fixture and explicit version; never infer equality from display owner/slug.
No cross-compatibility claim; both are outside v1.

collisions.issue: Twin-chat is described as if it were the complete neighborhood protocol.
collisions.resolution: Twin-chat is the inner envelope owned by the broader pinned neighborhood contract.

local_product.name: rapp-stack-cubby
local_product.truth: This working repository was absent from the public account inventory at the cutoff and is represented separately as the complete local product context.

evidence.snapshot_semantics: bounded non-atomic observation window; each head is paired with its own API response time
